notes: iPad 画質-chip zoom bug — real root cause + fix (2026-06). The 画質/FPS chip was the ONLY top control built as a <div onclick> (every other one is a <button>). On iOS a generic <div> is a double-tap-zoom target and touch-action doesn't reach its child <span>s, so tapping the chip (the tap lands on the 🖥/高/fps span) double-tap-zoomed the whole top button row — which is why the earlier <div>-level touch-action:manipulation didn't help and why this control 'reacted differently' from the others. Fix: the chip is now a real <button type=button> (appearance:none to keep the identical look) with touch-action:manipulation on it AND its spans. Native buttons don't double-tap-zoom and give a clean immediate tap, so the chip now behaves exactly like the neighbouring buttons. Verified: renders identically (97×23, same padding/border/radius), top row still one line on phone (297px). Builds on v0.0.17.
